How it works Features Security Share Get One Link
Accessibility

One Link is for every body.

A network for everyone should reach everyone. This page is a continuously updated statement of how we're building for that, what we test, what we know is still imperfect, and how to tell us when we miss.

Standard we target

WCAG 2.1 AA.

We aim for full Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.1 Level AA conformance on every page of this site. The same standard the EU Accessibility Act 2025, the UK Equality Act, and US Section 508 reference.

What this means in practice

Promises this site keeps.

Keyboard-first navigation

Every interactive element is reachable with Tab. Every focused element has a visible 2px ring in our brand cyan. A Skip to content link is the first focusable element on every page. Escape closes any open menu or dialog.

Screen-reader semantics

Real heading hierarchy (h1 to h3, no skipping). Real buttons for buttons, real links for links. aria-current marks the page you're on in the nav. aria-live announces the SAS code, OS detection, and other dynamic state.

Color and contrast

4.5:1 minimum on every text-over-background pair, including the dimmed metadata rows. Gradient calls-to-action have a solid fallback. Information is never carried by color alone.

Motion respected

prefers-reduced-motion: reduce disables the coherence-field background, the typing-cursor animations, and the auto-cycling demo timings. The site stays fully usable with motion off.

High-contrast supported

forced-colors: active (Windows High Contrast Mode) maps gradients to system tokens so call-to-action buttons remain legible. prefers-contrast: more lifts text and border tokens further.

Touch and pointer

Every tappable target is 44 by 44 pixels minimum, matching Apple Human Interface and Material Design floors. touch-action: manipulation removes the 300ms tap delay on older mobile browsers.

What we still have to fix

Honest list.

No site is fully accessible. Here is what we know is imperfect today, what we are working on, and what is intentional.

WebGPU coherence field

The background shader is decorative and is fully gated on prefers-reduced-motion plus a desktop pointer-fine + non-saveData check. No information is carried by it. Skipping it changes nothing.

QR scanning

The pair-by-QR live demo uses a visual QR code. We are working on a parallel one-tap copy-paste flow so users who do not use a camera can complete the same pairing.

Audio cues

Our ambient audio mode is opt-in only. Every interactive demo is fully usable without audio. We do not use audio to convey state.

How we test

Continuous, not one-shot.

Accessibility is verified on every change, not at a single audit. CI runs pa11y across every page. Real keyboard-only and screen-reader walkthroughs ship with major releases. The site source is open, so anyone can confirm.

Tell us when we miss

Let us know.

If anything on this site or in One Link is not accessible to you, we want to hear. Email weareone@oneunity.earth with the page and what broke. We aim to acknowledge within five days and fix as quickly as we can.

Last updated 2026-05-17.

One Link

A free, private network for messages, files, and devices. Owned by no one. Belongs to everyone.
